pi_items.source: Image analysis: Blobs (25K) sample image (256x254 pixels) thresholded at 128 yields 61 large connected components (blobs), which become Voronoi seeds. scipy.ndimage.label() and centroid detection used for analysis.

Voronoi tessellation simulation: Created Voronoi diagram from 61 blob centroids covering 65,024 pixel total area. Nearest-neighbor distance algorithm applied to each pixel. Resulting median cell area: 1023 pixels.
Voronoi tessellation simulation: 61 Voronoi cells over 65,024 pixel image. Median cell area is 1023 pixels, which is well within the expected maximum of 5000.
Voronoi cell area variation: Computed coefficient of variation (std_dev / mean) of all 61 Voronoi cell areas. Mean area: 1065.97 pixels, Std Dev: 320.96 pixels, CV = 320.96 / 1065.97 = 0.301.

Voronoi cell area variation: Coefficient of variation of Voronoi cell areas is 0.301, well within expected range of 0.1-2.0. This reflects natural variation in cell sizes due to non-uniform centroid distribution.
Path specification in task.json and setup_task.sh scripts. This is the expected output directory and filename.

privileged_info_summary: The Voronoi Spatial Analysis task operates on the ImageJ Blobs (25K) built-in sample image (256×254 pixels, 65,024 total pixels). Image analysis confirms ~61 distinct particles/blobs after standard thresholding. Voronoi tessellation from these 61 centroids yields: median cell area of 1,023 pixels (within expected 200-5,000 range), cell count of 61 (exceeds minimum of 20), and coefficient of variation of 0.301 (within expected 0.1-2.0 range). All metadata expectations are physically plausible and consistent with the actual image data.
